next_steps: Send urine Legionella antigen and PCR
Initiate renal replacement therapy for severe AKI, hyperkalemia, and acidosis
Continue broad-spectrum antibiotics covering Legionella (e.g., fluoroquinolone or azithromycin)
Perform lumbar puncture if mental status does not improve with metabolic correction
Obtain creatine kinase-MB and troponin to rule out cardiac involvement
Consult nephrology for potential kidney biopsy if renal function fails to recover
Perform bronchoalveolar lavage for culture and molecular testing if diagnosis remains elusive

differential_diagnoses: Autoimmune myositis (e.g., antisynthetase syndrome) triggered by infection
Acute interstitial nephritis due to supplement-induced toxicity
next_steps: Urgent urine Legionella antigen testing and sputum PCR/culture
Initiation of continuous renal replacement therapy (CRRT) for severe AKI and metabolic derangement
Continued coverage for Legionella (e.g., levofloxacin) while escalating to broader systemic coverage
Serum myositis-specific antibody panel (including Jo-1) given the extreme CK and family history
Echocardiogram to assess for myocardial involvement and exclude cardiomyopathy
Metabolic monitoring including serial phosphorus, calcium, uric acid, and CK levels
Bronchoalveolar lavage with atypical pathogen PCR panels if symptoms persist despite antibiotic coverage
